title: kengyilia carinata
intro: Kengyilia carinata, also known as the "Tibetan wheat grass" or "Nagdari", is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have anti-cancer and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Kengyilia carinata include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically for skin conditions.
